title: Gymshark Review: A Style Writer’s Honest Take On The Brand
snippet:
IF YOU’VE SPENT any amount of time on fitness TikTok, chances are you've already heard of Gymshark. The UK-based activewear brand has a devoted following thanks to its mix of trendy designs, gym-ready performance fabrics, and prices that feel a little more approachable than some of the bigger names in the category, like lululemon or Alo. I’ve been wearing Gymshark’s workout clothes since my college years, and I get the hype.

SHOP GYMSHARK [...] My all-time favorite item from the brand is the Soft Sculpt Legging. It was my first purchase from Gymshark as a workout newbie, and it remains in my legging rotation to this day (which, as a Women’s Health style writer, says a lot). It feels super supportive around the waist and hips without squeezing the life out of me, and the waistband never rolls down—a huge legging pet peeve of mine. The Everyday Seamless Cropped Tank is another favorite: It’s soft, stretchy, and supportive enough that I don’t need to wear a sports bra underneath for low- to medium-impact workouts. It also looks super polished thanks to the no seams, racerback design, and elongated V-neck. I wear it with the Soft Sculpt Legging a lot, and also on its own with sweats or lounge shorts on a casual athleisure day. [...] Whether you're looking for new pieces to wear during your toughest workouts or simply want activewear that looks as stylish as the rest of your wardrobe, Gymshark has you covered (with a whole lot of brattitude). I’m a big fan of everything I’ve tried from the activewear label, from their durable materials to stylish designs to prices that don’t make my wallet cry. Having tried tons of different workout clothing brands, I can confidently say Gymshark is worth your coin.

title: lululemon Launches Its First-Ever Sweat-Concealing Technology For High-Sweat Activities
snippet:
VANCOUVER, British Columbia--(BUSINESS WIRE)--lululemon (NASDAQ: LULU) today announced the launch of its first-ever sweat-concealing technology designed for high-sweat activities. The new technology, which will live under lululemon’s ShowZero™platform, conceals higher volumes of sweat, while ensuring enhanced breathability, moisture management, and an incredibly lightweight feel, to support the most demanding, high-sweat activities.

The new ShowZerotechnology was developed in collaboration with lululemon Ambassador Frances Tiafoe and will be featured in his custom kit for the BNP Paribas Open. The kit features a Burgundy Bay colourway, with a vertical stripe design on the shorts. [...] The new ShowZero is a yarn technology that changes how light interacts with the fabric and eliminates the absorption of light when wet, making sweat virtually invisible. In addition to concealing sweat, the technology offers enhanced comfort with its lightweight feel, breathability, and advanced wicking to facilitate quick drying.

lululemon plans to bring new ShowZero products to guests later this year.

The launch of ShowZero for high-sweat activities expands on lululemon’s sweat-concealing offerings. In summer 2024, lululemon introduced ShowZero for golf, in collaboration with PGA golfer and lululemon Ambassador Min Woo Lee.

title: lululemon Studio Review 2026 | Garage Gym Reviews
snippet:
## Tech Specs

The lululemon Studio requires a subscription to function. When you purchase your lululemon Studio, you’ll be prompted to sign up for the app membership, where all of the fitness content lives. The subscription costs $39 per month, but still cheaper than a traditional gym membership at most places. Even if you finance the device itself, you’re looking at less than $70 per month until you pay it off.

## lululemon Studio Review FAQs

### Can you use the lululemon Studio without a subscription?

Unfortunately, no. You must pay a $39 monthly subscription cost to access all its features and personal training classes.

### Is lululemon Studio a good workout?

The lululemon Studio has a variety of workout classes that challenge some longtime fitness enthusiasts on our team, so yes it can be a very good workout for people of all fitness levels.

### Is the MIRROR owned by Lululemon?

Yes, Lululemon acquired MIRROR in July 2020.

### Can lululemon Studio trainers see you? [...] RELATED: Best Smart Home Gym Equipment

One particular feature I love about the lululemon Studio is that you can toggle everything you see on-screen. If you don’t want to see the little community bubbles with other people’s usernames, you can toggle that off. You can also remove stats like calorie burn and heart rate.

## lululemon Studio Price

The lululemon Studio typically costs around $1,500 (just for the unit), but there are often sales where you can get it much cheaper than that. You’ll also have to pay a $250 delivery and installation fee.

## lululemon Studio Design

The studio has a sleek, futuristic design that makes it look like…well, a mirror. Unlike other workout mirrors, however, it is not a touchscreen.

Check if the brand uses recycled materials, publishes information about their factories, and holds third-party certifications (like Fair Trade or GOTS). Look for brands rated highly by platforms like Good On You. Many sustainable brands are proud to explain their eco-friendly steps.

### Are Gymshark Alternatives As Durable?

Not all gym clothes are created equal. If you do heavy lifting, look for compression leggings and tops with reinforced seams. Runners should focus on lightweight, sweat-wicking shirts and shorts with pockets. Yoga lovers need stretchy, soft fabrics that move with every pose.

### 2. Check Sizing Charts And Read Reviews

Brand sizing can be inconsistent. Always check size charts before buying, and read customer reviews for insights on fit, feel, and quality. Some brands run small, others large.

 Asos launches its first augmented reality feature

# Asos launches its first augmented reality feature

Retail

By Danielle Wightman-Stone

loading...

Asos has launched an experimental new augmented reality feature on its app, called Virtual Catwalk, which will test run on Asos Design products.

The new shopping experience marks the online fashion retailer’s first augmented reality (AR) experience and aims to offer customers a new way of viewing its products in real life.

The feature, developed in partnership with London-based augmented reality firm, HoloMe, is available now through the Asos app on iOS 11.3 devices and above globally and will be trialled across 100 new-in Asos Design products. [...] All customers need to do is point their smartphone camera at any suitable flat surface and click the ‘AR’ button on the product page in-app, and they will be able to view models as if they are walking in front of them.

Janosch Amstutz, chief executive at HoloMe, said in a statement: “By allowing the consumer to bring mobile shopping into their own physical space, we can create a more intimate buying experience. We are excited to see how our technology can be used as a new way to communicate to the customer.”

The test is part of Asos’ ongoing experimentation with new technology, in recent months, it has launched an Action on Google Assistant, allowing customers to shop with just their voice, and has introduced an AI-driven Fit Assistant to help customers get the right size, first time. [...] The online retailer is also trialling other AR features, including a tool which allows customers to view products on different size models, so customers can get a better sense of how something might fit their body shape.

# ASOS Launches ASOS Stylist App in ChatGPT

STOCKHOLM, SE / ACCESS Newswire / May 20, 2026 / Bambuser AB (STO:BUSER)(FRA:5JL) - ASOS, the go-to fashion destination, has launched ASOS Stylist, an app in ChatGPT, bringing fashion discovery through products and video content to customers in the UK and US.

The ASOS Stylist app helps customers discover ASOS products directly in ChatGPT and shop them on ASOS.com. Built using video commerce platform Bambuser, the app goes beyond today's AI shopping tools, which are primarily limited to text and static images. This is part of ASOS' ambition to make fashion discovery faster, easier, and more inspiring for customers, wherever they choose to browse. [...] ASOS Stylist uses Bambuser's new Intelligence Layer capability and video player widget to transform ASOS' product catalogue and video library into structured, machine-readable data that can be processed, retrieved, and returned, by large language models (LLMs) in real time as shoppable videos. ### 19. Nike Training

Nike Training has long been a leader in athletic wear. Their gym line includes everything from leggings to tanks.

### 20. Under Armour

Under Armour is known for durable, performance-focused gym clothes. They offer compression shirts, shorts, and leggings.

 Strengths: Excellent sweat-wicking, long-lasting fabrics.

 Range: Good for men and women. [...] ## Where To Shop For Gymshark Alternatives

 Official brand websites: Usually have the best selection and return policies.
 Large retailers: Sites like ASOS, Amazon, and Nordstrom carry multiple brands.
 Boutique fitness stores: Some local shops stock smaller brands you won’t find online.

Buying directly from the brand is usually safest for sizing and authenticity. Always check for shipping costs and return policies before you order.

## Caring For Your Gym Clothes

To make your gym clothes last longer, follow these tips:

 Wash in cold water: Hot water can damage stretchy fabrics.
 Avoid fabric softener: It can coat the fibers and reduce sweat-wicking abilities.
 Air dry: High heat from dryers can weaken elastic and cause shrinkage.
 Wash inside out: This protects colors and prints. [...] Best picks: Gym tees, tanks, and joggers for men.

 Product line: Gym leggings, shorts, and hoodies for both men and women.
 Durability: Clothes hold up well to heavy use.
 Style: Edgy, with bold logos and colors.

### 15. Tala

If you care about sustainability, Tala offers eco-friendly activewear with the same flattering fits as Gymshark.

 Materials: Recycled plastics and natural fibers.
 Fit: Sculpting and supportive.
 Mission: Focused on ethical production and low impact on the environment.

title: adidas and Satisfy Come Together to Announce Multi-Season ...
snippet:
adidas and technical running brand SATISFY have announced a multi-season partnership with product releases planned throughout 2026 and beyond.The new collaboration was launched through a unique running experience in the Sonoran Desert and the reveal of their debut drop, the ADIZERO ADIOS PRO 4 SATISFY.

Bringing together adidas’ race-day performance innovation and SATISFY’s culture-first creative perspective, the collaboration explores a new expression of contemporary running culture - where elite performance, identity, community, and self-expression intersect. [...] Features include an asymmetric colour fade inspired by mismatching shoes worn in skate culture and DIY spray-paint techniques,while the matte silver Energy Rods draw inspiration from off-road buggies. The limited-edition silhouette retains the proven performance innovations of the ADIZERO ADIOS PRO franchise including:full-length Lightstrike Pro foam for energy return, race-tuned Continental rubber grip with LIGHTTRAXION technology, and carbon-infused Energy Rods 2.0.

Launch details:

The ADIZERO ADIOS PRO 4 SATISFY will be available from May 22, 2026, on satisfyrunning.com and on May 25, 2026,(depending on geography)on the CONFIRMED APP, atadidas.com, and via theadidas flagship app.​

Product Highlights: [...] Product Highlights:

   FULL-LENGTH LIGHTSTRIKE PRO - Our most responsive foam runs the length of the midsole, providing energy return for your race-day stride.
   LIGHTLOCK UPPER - A performance-tuned upper with one-way stretch adapts to your foot for a locked-in fit built for racing.
   HYPERZONED GRIP - Experience ultimate control at speed with hyperzoned grip, derived by pressure map data, with race-tuned Continental rubber and LIGHTTRAXION technology.
   CARBON-FIBRE ENERGY RODS 2.0 - Carbon-fibre Energy Rods provide a seamless heal-to-toe transition, built for racing.

title: Are Gymshark Clothes Worth It? Honest Review and Buyer Guide
snippet:
### Style That Crosses Over

Because Gymshark clothes look good, people wear them outside the gym. A pair of leggings with a crop top can be worn to brunch or a casual event. The branding is visible but not too flashy for most tastes.

## Comparing Gymshark To Other Brands

Is Gymshark better than Nike, Lululemon, or Adidas? Here’s a side-by-side look at how Gymshark stacks up.

| Brand | Price Range (USD) | Main Strength | Common Weakness |
 ---  --- |
| Gymshark | $30–$70 | Trendy designs, good value | Sizing inconsistency |
| Lululemon | $80–$120 | Premium quality, comfort | High price |
| Nike | $40–$100 | Durable, wide selection | Less trendy |
| Adidas | $35–$90 | Reliable, sporty | Basic designs | [...] When you touch a Gymshark legging or top, it usually feels smooth and lightweight, sometimes slightly slick. The seamless ranges are especially soft. Compared to cheaper brands, Gymshark fabrics feel thicker and more supportive. However, some collections are lighter and might feel less durable.

### Moisture-wicking And Breathability

For sweaty workouts, moisture-wicking is key. Gymshark’s blends do a good job of pulling sweat away from the skin. This helps prevent chafing and keeps you cool. Their “Adapt” and “Vital” lines are praised for breathability during HIIT or cardio. The downside is that lighter colors may show sweat marks more easily.

### Durability And Wash Tests [...] ### How Does Gymshark Sizing Compare To Other Brands?

Gymshark sizing can run smaller, especially in seamless lines. If you are between sizes, it is safer to size up for leggings. Tops and hoodies are closer to standard sizing, but always check the size chart for each item.

### Do Gymshark Clothes Shrink Or Lose Shape After Washing?

If you follow care instructions—cold wash, no dryer—Gymshark clothes keep their shape well. Machine drying can cause shrinkage or damage elastic fibers, so air drying is best.

### Is Gymshark Suitable For High-intensity Workouts?

Yes, most Gymshark collections handle high-intensity workouts well, especially “Vital Seamless” and “Adapt. ” They wick sweat and stay in place. For daily, heavy wear, durability may be less than top luxury brands.

What we like: Icebreaker uses merino wool to create natural performance alternatives for a synthetic-free workout. Its activewear, which includes sports bras, t-shirts, tanks, shorts, outer layers, and leggings, is designed to be versatile and long-lasting. (Learn why merino wool isn’t itchy and makes for good activewear.) The brand fosters long-term relationships with its merino sheep farmers and traces its supply chain to ensure ethical practices from beginning to end.

Our review: Icebreaker makes amazing hiking layers, which often aren’t itchy and work well even on a hot day, plus are minimalist enough to pass for everyday wear. The sports bra is super supportive and stylish as well.

What we like: Community clothing makes 100% plastic-free activewear, including shorts, tanks, and shirts. It uses organic cotton and cotton thread and manufacturers in the U.K. from spinning to garment making. It sources its organic cotton for its sports wear from a family-owned farm in California. In addition to creating safe and durable clothing, the brand is dedicated to increasing textile jobs in the U.K.

What we like: Tripulse designs timeless and sustainable performance apparel made from innovative natural fabrics instead of plastics. Its 100% Tencel tops are hypoallergenic and odor resistant with bluesign and Oeko-Tex-certified dyes and finishes. Its Tencel is sourced in Austria, produced in a GOTS-certified partner factory, and all products are designed and ethically made in Europe within Reach standards. Some high-performance garments contain a percentage of Roica, a compostable version of elastane.

Under Armour, Inc. is an American sportswear company that manufactures footwear and apparel headquartered in Baltimore, Maryland, United States.

## History

### 20th century

Under Armour was founded on September 25, 1996, by Kevin Plank, a then-24 year old former special teams captain of the University of Maryland football team. Plank initially began the business from his grandmother's basement in Washington, D.C. He spent his time traveling along the East Coast with nothing but apparel in the trunk of his car. His first team sale came at the end of 1996 with a $17,000 sale. From his grandmother's Washington, D.C.-area rowhouse, Plank moved to Baltimore.

title: Instagram
snippet:
The middle tier of the chart reveals a sharp contrast in Millennial engagement, where Under Armour pulls ahead with 42.9% of its audience versus Gymshark’s 37.2% share. We see Millennials acting as Under Armour’s digital anchor cohort, balancing its outreach to emerging Gen Z segments. For both brands, maintaining Millennial loyalty while cultivating younger shoppers will be key to sustaining e-commerce efficiency and repeat purchasing. [...] Gymshark vs. Under Armour—Gymshark emerges as the clear Gen Z web engagement leader, with 53.6% of its forecast 2026 audience coming from 14 to 29 year olds compared with Under Armour’s 40.2% share in the same cohort. The brand’s digital-first DNA and TikTok-heavy strategy align with its broader target of 18 to 34 year olds, who currently drive roughly 70% of Gymshark revenue and interact heavily with social and DTC channels. [...] At the top of the age curve, the lower-ranked Gen X segment highlights Under Armour’s broader generational spread, with 16.9% of its forecast web audience coming from 46 to 61 year olds compared with Gymshark’s 9.2%. We interpret the generational skew as a warning sign for incumbents: brands that do not materially grow Gen Z share risk long-term digital erosion even if older cohorts remain stable.   
  
The signal is clear: future category winners will balance performance credibility with youth-oriented, creator-led commerce that keeps Gen Z at the center of web audience growth.
